name: Cookware (pots and pans)
summary: Pots, pans, skillets, and cookware sets — stainless, cast iron, nonstick, carbon steel.

evidence.claim: PFOA was phased out of nonstick cookware in the US by 2013. Modern 'PFOA-free' is the default, not a differentiator. However, PTFE-based nonstick (most brands) still releases fumes above 500°F.
'Tri-ply' or 'clad' cookware construction (stainless outer + aluminum core) distributes heat more evenly than single-layer stainless; thickness of the aluminum core matters more than the ply count.

confabulationPatterns.reality: PFOA has been phased out since 2013 (E1) — this is baseline. PTFE-based nonstick (almost all brands) still releases fumes above 500°F and is not safe for all cooking.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is PFOA-free framed as a premium feature? It's baseline.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Ply count matters less than aluminum-core thickness (E2). Some 'tri-ply' has 0.8mm core; some 'single-layer' with thick encapsulated disc performs identically.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the aluminum core thickness in mm?

confabulationPatterns.reality: All PTFE-based nonstick degrades with use (1-5 years typical depending on treatment). 'Forever' claims are marketing; ceramic-coating nonstick degrades faster than PTFE.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the realistic lifespan of the nonstick coating?

counterfeitSignals.signal: All-Clad or Le Creuset >50% off from marketplace seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — counterfeit high-end cookware with inferior materials is widespread

compatibilityQuestions: Induction cooktop? Requires ferromagnetic base (magnetic stainless, cast iron, carbon steel).
Gas or electric? Gas loves carbon steel / cast iron; electric benefits from thick clad aluminum core.

repairability.notes: Cast iron lasts a century; nonstick is disposable. Stainless can be reconditioned by professional re-polishing.
